Two Palestinians killed by Israeli fire since dawn
articles.summary: Two Palestinians killed by Israeli fire since dawn Two Palestinians have been killed in Israeli attacks in southern Gaza since dawn, Wafa news agency reported. According to medical sources, the unidentified victim was shot dead by Israeli forces east of Khan Younis in southern Gaza. Earlier, an Israeli drone strike killed 15-year-old Riyad Naji Abu Nimer. The latest figures bring the overall toll of Palestinians killed in Israeli attacks in Gaza since the ceasefire was imposed in October 2025 to 831, with another 2,342 wounded. At least 767 bodies have been recovered from the rubble.

articles.summary: Iraq oil exports could resume within a week, minister says Iraq’s Deputy Oil Minister Basim Mohammed said Iraq could restore oil output and exports to normal levels within seven days once the crisis over the Strait of Hormuz ends. “Iraq can restore oil output and exports to normal levels within seven days of the crisis ending,” he said. He added that production currently stands at about 1.5 million barrels per day, with roughly 200,000 barrels exported via Ceyhan in Turkey. articles.title: OPEC+ meets to set quotas after UAE exit
articles.summary: OPEC+ meets to set quotas after UAE exit Seven members of OPEC+ are meeting to decide oil production levels for the first time since the United Arab Emirates left the group, Al Jazeera reported. The UAE formally exited on 2 May, after deciding on 28 April to withdraw over dissatisfaction with production limits. According to traders cited in the report, the countries are expected to increase output by about 188,000 barrels per day. The meeting comes at a time of market volatility, as producers weigh supply levels following the UAE’s departure.

articles.title: Iran sets timeline, demands guarantees in peace proposal
articles.summary: Iran sets timeline, demands guarantees in peace proposal A 14-point proposal from Iran sets out a framework to end the war, according to Tasnim News Agency. The plan responds to a US proposal and calls for resolving all issues within 30 days, prioritising a permanent end to the conflict rather than extending a temporary ceasefire. It includes demands for guarantees against future attacks, a withdrawal of United States forces from around Iran, the release of frozen assets, lifting sanctions and war reparations. The proposal also calls for ending hostilities across the region, including in Leban

articles.title: US to supply Israel with precision weapon kits, report says
articles.summary: US to supply Israel with precision weapon kits, report says The Israeli newspaper Haaretz reported that the United States will supply Israel with about 10,000 advanced precision weapon kits worth nearly $1bn. The systems are designed to convert air-to-ground missiles into precision-guided munitions capable of targeting aerial threats, including some drones. The report said the systems could improve interception capabilities but would not be effective against smaller, vision-based drones that have posed challenges to Israeli forces in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: South African Wine Estates Become Focus for Global Buyers
articles.summary: Global investors are pouring money into commercial wineries in and around Cape Town, even as wine sales slow worldwide. In a post on X, the firm identified the vessel as “HUGE” and said it was last seen off Sri Lanka and is now transiting the Lombok Strait towards the Riau Archipelago. Trump to review Iran's 14-point plan, warns strikes could resume
articles.summary: Trump to review Iran's 14-point plan, warns strikes could resume US President Donald Trump said there is a “possibility” that strikes against Iran could resume if tensions escalate. Speaking to reporters in Florida, he said: “If Iran misbehaves or does something bad there is a possibility it could happen… we’ll see,” adding that the US is “doing very well” and that Tehran wants to make a deal because they are “decimated”. Trump also said, “They are having a hard time figuring out who their leader is, they don’t know who their leader is,” adding that their former leader Ali Khamenei “is gone”.

articles.summary: 'Aparthied Israel': UN rapporteur calls for release of Gaza flotilla activists UN special rapporteur on the occupied Palestinian territory, Francesca Albanese, condemned Israel over the interception of the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla in international waters, calling for the release of two activists. In a post on X, she wrote: “It is an abomination that Apartheid Israel, while continuing its genocide undisturbed, is allowed to patrol European waters, seize boats, kidnap people. Hostage taking is a crime." Palestinian and Global Sumud Flotilla hostages must be released now, she said.

articles.title: Iran presents 14-point plan to US to end war, Washington yet to respond
articles.summary: Iran presents 14-point plan to US to end war, Washington yet to respond Iran has put forward a 14-point proposal to the United States aimed at ending the war, according to the semi-official Tasnim News Agency. The move follows a reported nine-point plan from Washington, with both sides outlining competing approaches to a possible settlement. Tasnim said the US proposal called for a ceasefire with a two-month timeline, while Tehran is pushing for a faster resolution.
